# ScreenHand

> Give AI eyes and hands on your desktop.

ScreenHand is an open-source MCP (Model Context Protocol) server that lets AI assistants like Claude see your screen, click buttons, type text, and control any application on macOS and Windows.

## Key Facts

- 30+ automation tools
- ~50ms per UI action via native Accessibility APIs
- ~10ms Chrome DevTools Protocol operations
- ~600ms screenshot + OCR
- Works with Claude Desktop, Claude Code, Cursor, Windsurf, OpenAI Codex CLI
- MIT licensed, free and open-source
- Runs entirely locally, no cloud dependency
- Supports macOS 12+ and Windows 10 (1809+)
- Built-in learning memory with O(1) recall
- Anti-detection stealth mode for bot-protected sites
- Platform playbooks with pre-built selectors and error solutions

## Links

- Website: https://screenhand.com
- GitHub: https://github.com/manushi4/screenhand
- npm: https://www.npmjs.com/package/screenhand
